title: Machaerina anceps
taxonomy: Machaerina anceps is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family consists of approximately 5,000 species of plants, including sedge, papyrus, and sawgrass. Machaerina anceps is further categorized within the subfamily Cyperoideae, which comprises most of the sedge species. The genus Machaerina is also a part of this subfamily, containing around 60 species. These plants are often found in wet environments and are native to various parts of the world.
uses: Machaerina anceps is reported to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine.
